Log in / Sign up
English (US)

Meaning of good in English

good
adjective
us
/ɡʊd/ uk
/ɡʊd/
better | best

good adjective (PLEASANT/SATISFACTORY)

Add to word list Add to word list
A1
very satisfactory, enjoyable, pleasant, or interesting:
a good book
Did you have a good time at the party?
The weather has been really good for the time of year.
I've just had some very good news.
it is good to It's so good to see you after all this time!
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
giving pleasure and holding your attention
  • goodIt's a good film overall.
  • enjoyableWe had an enjoyable time with Bob and Helen.
  • pleasant The weather is pleasant but not too hot.
  • interestingThe history of the place was really interesting.
  • niceHave a nice day!
See more results »
More examplesFewer examples
  • You'll like Rosie - she's good company.
  • Such a noisy environment was not conducive to a good night's sleep.
  • The empty roads make this area good biking country.
  • The good thing about the bike is that it dismantles if you want to put it in the back of the car.
  • I was having such a good time I was reluctant to leave.
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Good, better and best
  • (as) good as gold idiom
  • all is well idiom
  • at the best of times idiom
  • be off the hook idiom
  • be the last word in something idiom
  • break
  • cut
  • gold standard
  • heteronormative
  • high-quality
  • knock something into a cocked hat idiom
  • leave someone/something in the dust idiom
  • miles idiom
  • second
  • so much the better idiom
  • sound
  • super good
  • topper
  • unexcelled
  • unsurpassed
See more results »
 have a good one
informal
used when saying goodbye to someone:
"Maybe I'll see you next week." - "Yeah, have a good one." - "You too, bye."
See more

good adjective (HEALTHY)

A1
healthy or well:
I didn't go into work because I wasn't feeling too good.
"How's your mother?" "She's good, thanks."
 I'm good informal
used as a general reply when someone greets you:
"How are you doing?" "I'm good, thanks."
See more
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Animal physiology: fit & healthy
  • acrobatically
  • agility
  • agree with something phrasal verb
  • amazonian
  • apple
  • buff
  • hale and hearty
  • in the pink idiom
  • keep fit
  • limber
  • lithe
  • lithely
  • match fit
  • match fitness
  • muscled
  • muscleman
  • muscly
  • muscular
  • rude
  • sporty
See more results »

good adjective (HIGH QUALITY)

A1
of a high quality or level:
She speaks very good French.
I've heard it's a very good school.
as good as The apple pie was as good as the one my grandmother used to make.
good reputation This restaurant has a good reputation.
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
of high quality
  • goodBuy a really good foundation and cultivate a healthy glow.
  • good/high qualityWe have a real commitment to high-quality ingredients.
  • qualityWe are working hard to produce a quality product.
  • fineThey have a well-stocked cellar full of fine wines.
  • superiorThe food and service here are superior to any other restaurant in the city.
See more results »
used to express praise:
Good man! Splendid catch.
More examplesFewer examples
  • Her work is sometimes good, but the problem is she's not consistent.
  • We need someone who can write good copy for our publicity department.
  • With a good telescope, you can see craters on the moon.
  • It's important for children to get a good education.
  • The design is so good it's sure to set the pattern for many others.
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Good, better and best
  • (as) good as gold idiom
  • all is well idiom
  • at the best of times idiom
  • be off the hook idiom
  • be the last word in something idiom
  • break
  • cut
  • gold standard
  • heteronormative
  • high-quality
  • knock something into a cocked hat idiom
  • leave someone/something in the dust idiom
  • miles idiom
  • second
  • so much the better idiom
  • sound
  • super good
  • topper
  • unexcelled
  • unsurpassed
See more results »

You can also find related words, phrases, and synonyms in the topics:

Praising and applauding

good adjective (SUCCESSFUL)

A1
successful, or able to do something well:
Kate's a good cook.
good at She's very good at math.
They have a good relationship.
good with She's very good with children.
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
good at doing something because of practice
  • skillfulShe's a skillful driver.
  • skilledHe's a skilled mechanic.
  • good atShe's very good at dealing with people.
  • ableShe's a very able student.
  • giftedShe's a gifted musician.
  • talentedHe's a very talented actor.
See more results »
 be no good (also be not any/much good)
B2
to be of low quality or not useful:
Shoes are no good if they let in water.
See more
 get off to a good start
to begin an activity successfully:
I didn't get off to a very good start this morning - I'd been at work five minutes and my computer stopped working!
See more
More examplesFewer examples
  • A good teacher has an easy authority over a class.
  • The design certainly looks good on paper.
  • A good architect takes into account the building's surroundings.
  • Do you know a good recipe for whole wheat bread?
  • Painting steel is a good way to rustproof it.
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Successful (things or people)
  • a head of steam idiom
  • achiever
  • alive
  • alpha female
  • alpha male
  • arrive
  • ascendant
  • crest
  • good with someone/something
  • greatest hits
  • have (got) it made idiom
  • have something in your pocket idiom
  • have the world at your feet idiom
  • rail
  • smash
  • storming
  • successful
  • successfully
  • superachiever
  • superhit
See more results »

good adjective (KIND)

A1
kind or helpful:
a good friend
it is good of It's good of you to offer to help.
good to He's very good to his mother.
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
a kind person
  • kindYou'll never meet a kinder person.
  • niceHe's such a nice guy.
  • sweetShe's the sweetest little girl in the whole class.
  • good toJay's mother has been very good to us.
  • good-heartedHe's a good-hearted kid who loves to help out.
See more results »
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
morally right
  • goodShe is a good woman who goes out of her way to help others.
  • goodHe believes that children should be encouraged to do good deeds.
  • virtuousI believe that humans are, despite their flaws, capable of virtuous conduct.
  • righteousHe refused to stray from the righteous path.
  • honestHe was a hardworking, honest family man who was only trying to do his duty.
See more results »
 be so good as to formal (also be good enough to)
used to make a polite request:
Be so good as to close the door when you leave.
See more
 do (someone) a good turn old-fashioned
to do something kind that helps someone else:
I was only trying to do her a good turn.
See more
More examplesFewer examples
  • I think she takes advantage of his good nature.
  • It was very good of you to come round at such short notice.
  • My main goal in life is to be a good husband and father.
  • Be a good boy and eat up your vegetables.
  • She's a good warm-hearted woman.
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Kind & thoughtful
  • accommodating
  • accommodatingly
  • attentive
  • attentively
  • attentiveness
  • diplomacy
  • diplomatic
  • diplomatically
  • eunoia
  • fatherly
  • maternal
  • mean well idiom
  • motherly
  • neighborliness
  • tactfully
  • the milk of human kindness idiom
  • thoughtful
  • thoughtfully
  • thoughtfulness
  • unmalicious
See more results »

good adjective (MORALLY RIGHT)

B1
morally right or based on religious principles:
She led a good life.
Try to set a good example for the children.
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
morally right
  • goodShe is a good woman who goes out of her way to help others.
  • goodHe believes that children should be encouraged to do good deeds.
  • virtuousI believe that humans are, despite their flaws, capable of virtuous conduct.
  • righteousHe refused to stray from the righteous path.
  • honestHe was a hardworking, honest family man who was only trying to do his duty.
See more results »
More examplesFewer examples
  • You should be setting a good example to your younger brother.
  • He was a good, honest man.
  • She came from a good family.
  • The preacher believed that he was spreading the good word.
  • The vicar often quoted from the Good Book.
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Virtue and moral good
  • altruism
  • angel
  • angelic
  • angelically
  • be as pure as the driven snow idiom
  • justifiable
  • justifiably
  • justified
  • justified in something/doing something
  • loftiness
  • righteousness
  • rightful
  • rightfully
  • rightly
  • rightness
  • unstained
  • upliftment
  • uprightly
  • uprightness
  • upstanding
See more results »

You can also find related words, phrases, and synonyms in the topics:

Religious concepts: holiness

good adjective (POSITIVE)

A1
having a positive or useful effect, especially on the health:
Make sure you eat plenty of good fresh food.
good for Too much sugar in your diet isn't good for you.
it is good for someone to do something It's good for old people to stay active if they can.
More examplesFewer examples
  • Cheers! Your good health.
  • Fresh fruit is very good for you.
  • This shampoo is very good for your hair.
  • Staring at the computer screen is not good for your eyes.
  • What's good for a sore throat?
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Useful or advantageous
  • (as) good as gold idiom
  • advantageous
  • advantageously
  • all-singing
  • amiss
  • fecundity
  • fodder
  • fodder for someone/something
  • foil
  • fruitfully
  • non-expendable
  • non-frivolous
  • non-productive
  • not to be sniffed at idiom
  • novelty value
  • versatile
  • versatility
  • viability
  • viable
  • wholesome
See more results »

You can also find related words, phrases, and synonyms in the topics:

Animal physiology: fit & healthy

good adjective (BEHAVIOR)

A2
A good child or animal behaves well:
If you're a good boy at the doctor's, I'll take you swimming afterward.
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
well behaved
  • well behavedOur kids are normally well behaved.
  • goodBe a good boy and sit quietly.
  • as good as goldThe babysitter said Arlo had been as good as gold.
  • angelJulian is a little angel.
  • obedientHe always tried his best to be an obedient son.
See more results »
able to be trusted:
Her credit is good (= she can be trusted to pay her debts).
More examplesFewer examples
  • I'll just have to take you home if you can't be good, children.
  • Settle down now, that's a good dog.
  • You've been such a good girl this morning - I'm very proud of you.
  • You are in fourth grade now - surely you can be good for just a few minutes while your teacher is called away!
  • He was always a good child and never any trouble.
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Behaving well
  • (as) good as gold idiom
  • angelically
  • be on your best behavior idiom
  • behave
  • breeding
  • child
  • demure
  • demurely
  • demureness
  • do a good deed
  • example
  • gracious
  • non-disruptive
  • polite
  • rail
  • set an example idiom
  • stay on the rails idiom
  • super good
  • well mannered
  • well spoken
See more results »

You can also find related words, phrases, and synonyms in the topics:

Loyal, faithful & dependable

good adjective (SUITABLE)

A2
suitable, convenient, or satisfactory:
When would be a good time to phone?
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Suitable and acceptable
  • able
  • acceptability
  • acceptable
  • acceptably
  • accepted
  • convenience
  • correctly
  • correctness
  • decent
  • decently
  • do the decent thing
  • felicity
  • just what the doctor ordered idiom
  • legitimate
  • pg.
  • proper
  • properly
  • quality
  • re-eligibility
  • worthy
See more results »

good adjective (LARGE)

C1 [ before noun ]
used to emphasize the large number, amount, or level of something:
We've walked a good distance today.
There was a good-sized crowd at the airport waiting for the plane to land.
a good few Not all of his movies have been successful - there were a good few (= several) failures in the early years.
You'll need a good length of rope to secure this properly.
You have a good cry and you'll feel better after.
There's a good chance the operation will be successful.
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
greater than the average size or amount
  • bigThey live in a big house in the country.
  • largeA large number of people were crowded into the room.
  • greatThe party was a great success.
  • enormousThey bought an enormous house in the suburbs.
See more results »
 a good deal of
B2
much:
The new law met with a good deal of opposition at the local level.
See more
 a good ... C2 (also a good ...'s)
more than:
It's a good half hour's walk to the station from here.
See more
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Big and quite big
  • appreciable
  • baronial
  • bigly
  • bulky
  • fullness
  • grandiose
  • grandiosely
  • grandly
  • grandness
  • greatness
  • growthy
  • on the large, small, etc. side idiom
  • outsize
  • over-large
  • palatial
  • palatially
  • the bigger the better idiom
  • voluminously
  • XL
  • XXL
See more results »

good adjective (SATISFACTION)

A1
said when you are satisfied or pleased about something, or to show agreement with a decision:
Oh good, he's arrived at last.
Good, I'll tell her it's all arranged, then.
"The doctor says there's nothing to worry about." "Oh good."
Good, I've got the right person.
 I'm good
used to tell someone that you have everything that you need:
"More coffee?" "No, I'm good, thanks."
See more
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Words & phrases expressing agreement & acceptance
  • accha
  • achha
  • amen
  • amen to that idiom
  • as you wish/like idiom
  • beggar
  • couldn't agree more/less idiom
  • fair
  • hear
  • I know idiom
  • indeed
  • quite
  • should
  • size
  • very
  • why not...? idiom
  • wilco
  • will do idiom
  • yass
  • you can say that again! idiom
See more results »

You can also find related words, phrases, and synonyms in the topics:

Pleasure and happiness

Grammar

Good at, bad at
We use adjective + at to talk about things that we do well or badly: …

Idioms

be a good
all in good time
as good as
(as) good as gold
be (as) good as new
be as good as your word
be good for something
be good to go
be good, and if you can't be good, be careful
for good measure
More idioms
good and ...
good and proper
good for you!
good heavens/grief/gracious!
the good old days
good show
have a good innings
have a good war
in good faith
in good time
make good
make good on something
make good time
good
noun
us
/ɡʊd/ uk
/ɡʊd/

good noun (THINGS)

 goods [ plural ]
B1
things for sale, or the things that you own:
There is a 25 percent discount on all electrical goods until the end of the week.
UK
things, but not people, that are transported by railroad or road:
a goods train
See more

good noun (HELP)

B2 [ U ]
something that is an advantage or help to a person or situation:
a lot of good Even a small donation can do a lot of good.
for your/someone's own good I'm telling you for your own good.
More examplesFewer examples
  • Huge projects designed to aid poorer countries can sometimes do more harm than good.
  • The captain resigned for the good of the team.
  • He tried tightening the cable, but it didn't do any good.
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Good and excellent things
  • badass
  • be the best thing since sliced bread idiom
  • beatification
  • beaut
  • beauty
  • flagship
  • green flag
  • grouse
  • hidden depths
  • hidden gem
  • high-end
  • humdinger
  • keeper
  • peach
  • pull factor
  • queen
  • ripper
  • superstar
  • the real deal
  • the Rolls-Royce of something idiom
See more results »

good noun (HEALTH)

[ U ]
the state of being healthy or in a satisfactory condition:
for your/someone's own good You should stop smoking for your own good (= for your health).
for the good of someone’s health He goes running every day for the good of his health.
do more harm than good Modernizing historic buildings can often do more harm than good.
for the good of The decision has been postponed for the good of all concerned.
Synonym
benefit (ADVANTAGE)
 do someone good
B2
to improve someone's health or life:
do someone good to do something You can't work all the time - it does you good to go out and enjoy yourself sometimes.
See more
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Useful or advantageous
  • (as) good as gold idiom
  • advantageous
  • advantageously
  • all-singing
  • amiss
  • fecundity
  • fodder
  • fodder for someone/something
  • foil
  • fruitfully
  • non-expendable
  • non-frivolous
  • non-productive
  • not to be sniffed at idiom
  • novelty value
  • versatile
  • versatility
  • viability
  • viable
  • wholesome
See more results »

You can also find related words, phrases, and synonyms in the topics:

Animal physiology: fit & healthy

good noun (MORALLY RIGHT)

C2 [ U ]
that which is morally right:
There is an eternal struggle between good (= the force that produces morally right action) and evil.
for good Ambition can sometimes be a force for good.
 the good [ plural ]
all the people who are morally good
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Virtue and moral good
  • altruism
  • angel
  • angelic
  • angelically
  • be as pure as the driven snow idiom
  • justifiable
  • justifiably
  • justified
  • justified in something/doing something
  • loftiness
  • righteousness
  • rightful
  • rightfully
  • rightly
  • rightness
  • unstained
  • upliftment
  • uprightly
  • uprightness
  • upstanding
See more results »

You can also find related words, phrases, and synonyms in the topics:

People having good qualities

good noun (TIME)

 for good
C1
forever:
She's gone and this time it's for good.
See more

See also

the greater good

Grammar

Noun phrases: premodifiers (big, good, red)
Premodifiers consist of single adjectives, adjective phrases, single nouns and noun phrases which are used before the head in a noun phrase. …
Always: meaning
Always can mean ‘on every occasion’, ‘forever’ or ‘very frequently’. In these meanings we use it with simple tense forms: …
Always with continuous verb forms
We can use always with continuous verb forms to refer to regular events or states, especially ones which are problematic or which we do not like or want: …
Always: position
We most commonly use always in mid position, between the subject and main verb, after the modal verb or first auxiliary verb, or after main verb be: …
Always with can and could
We often use always with can and could to talk about possible solutions to problems: …
As always
We use as always to talk about one event which is seen as typical: …
Always, for good or for ever?
When we talk about things which will be permanent, we normally use for good, or, more formally, for ever (sometimes written as forever). We do not normally say for always: …

Idioms

be up to no good
come up with the goods
no good
to the good
what good is ...

be a good

idiom
B2
(UK also be a good job)
used to mean "it is lucky":
It's a good job they didn't go camping last weekend - the weather was awful.
Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and examples
having good things happen by chance
  • luckyTwo lucky winners will be treated to an all-expenses paid trip to the Bahamas!
  • fortunateIt was fortunate that he wasn't injured in the accident.
  • it's a good thingIt's a good thing we bought travel insurance for our trip since it was canceled.
  • it's a good jobUK It's a good job they didn't go camping this weekend - the weather's been awful.
  • luck outUS The Giants really lucked out in last night's game.
See more results »
SMART Vocabulary: related words and phrases
Good luck and bad luck
  • align
  • bad luck
  • bad omen
  • bad/hard/tough luck! idiom
  • be bad luck on someone idiom
  • fortunately
  • fortune cookie
  • fortune smiles on someone idiom
  • get the short end of the stick idiom
  • godsend
  • lucky
  • lucky escape
  • malediction
  • may the best man/person win! idiom
  • merciful
  • unfortunate
  • unfortunately
  • unluckily
  • unlucky
  • voodoo
See more results »

a good ...

phrase (also a good ...'s)
C2
more than:
It's a good half hour's walk to the station from here.
The police said a good 20 pounds of explosive were found during the raid.
a good many Driving through the deserted town, we saw a good many (= a lot of) burned-out houses.
It was late and the nearest town was still a good fifty miles away.
I shed a good many tears over that man in my youth.
See also
good adjective (LARGE)
(Definition of good from the Cambridge Advanced Learner's Dictionary & Thesaurus © Cambridge University Press)

good | Intermediate English

good
adjective
us
/ɡʊd/

good adjective (SATISFACTORY)

Add to word list Add to word list
comparative better us/ˈbet̬·ər/ | superlative best us/best/
of a kind that is pleasing or enjoyable, or of high quality:
Let’s go on a picnic tomorrow if the weather’s good.
That was a really good meal.
Dogs have a very good sense of smell.
Now would be a good time (= a suitable time) to talk to Andy about the promotion.
He’s a good (= able and skillful) swimmer.
Did they have a good time on their vacation?
She makes good money (= earns a high income) in her new job.

good adjective (MORALLY RIGHT)

comparative better us/ˈbet̬·ər/ | superlative best us/best/
morally right or admirable:
José is a genuinely good person.
If you’re a good boy (= if you behave well) at the dentist, I’ll buy you some ice cream later.
He’s always been good to his mother.
comparative better us/ˈbet̬·ər/ | superlative best us/best/
Good can be used as part of an exclamation:
Good heavens! You mean they still haven’t arrived?

good adjective (WITH GREETING)

[ not gradable ]
used in greetings

good adjective (FOR HEALTH)

comparative better us/ˈbet̬·ər/ | superlative best us/best/
useful for health, or in a satisfactory condition:
Make sure you eat plenty of good, fresh vegetables.

good adjective (LARGE)

[ not gradable ]
large in number or amount:
We had to walk a good way in the airport to reach our gate.
There was a good-sized crowd on hand.
There was a good deal of (= a lot of) discussion about how much the car was worth.

good

noun [ U ] us
/ɡʊd/
Even a small donation can do a lot of good.

Idioms

a good
a good many
good and
(as) good as new
good for someone
good riddance
(it’s a) good thing
no good

a good

idiom
this much or more of a specific amount:
It’s a good half hour’s walk to the stadium from here.
(Definition of good from the Cambridge Academic Content Dictionary © Cambridge University Press)

good | Business English

good
noun [ S ]
  ECONOMICS
uk
/ɡʊd/ us
Add to word list Add to word list
a product that is made to be sold:
The demand for a good influences its price.
See also
economic good
goods
Compare
commodity
(Definition of good from the Cambridge Business English Dictionary © Cambridge University Press)

Examples of good

good
Sharing the gym might be a good place to start.
From Slate Magazine
Maybe too much of a good thing really is too much.
From USA TODAY
If that's the scenario, then that's a good thing.
From USA TODAY
Are you still a good man if you're politically incorrect?
From ESPN
But fifty years is a pretty good run.
From Chicago Tribune
He's a very good strategic thinker and will tell you what needs to be said whether you want to hear him or not.
From USA TODAY
Do all the good we can, in all the ways we can, for all the people we can.
From The Hill
She might have been a little too naive for her own good, but she was a trusting soul.
From CBS Local
It's good for their moral and it helps them scrub.
From Phys.Org
And it was a good prospect for him.
From NPR
For her to show that she is solid in her adopted state, 10 points or better should be a good number.
From New York Post
We're good at making communities that are welcoming.
From New York Post
It's a good thing he is so helpful.
From Huffington Post
It worries me because you have to put your time in to be good at anything.
From The Atlantic
And what's summer without a good summer reading list?
From The Atlantic
These examples are from corpora and from sources on the web. Any opinions in the examples do not represent the opinion of the Cambridge Dictionary editors or of Cambridge University Press or its licensors.

Collocations with good

good

These are words often used in combination with good.

Click on a collocation to see more examples of it.

agricultural goods
However, these new workers in the city demand the agricultural good, pushing the price higher, and reducing the real wage further.
From the Cambridge English Corpus  
baked good
Crumb softeners are added to bread to reduce or delay staling of the finished baked good.
From
Wikipedia
This example is from Wikipedia and may be reused under a CC BY-SA license.
 
basic good
As a basic good, household energy is very important to the poor: the urban poor, in particular, are vulnerable when energy prices increase.
From the Cambridge English Corpus  
These examples are from corpora and from sources on the web. Any opinions in the examples do not represent the opinion of the Cambridge Dictionary editors or of Cambridge University Press or its licensors.

  • Good Book
  • good day

Idioms and phrases

  • good and idiom
  • good for someone idiom
  • a good many idiom
  • good riddance idiom
  • (it’s a) good thing idiom
  • no good idiom
See all idioms and phrases

Word of the Day

lol

US
/ˌel.oʊˈel/

abbreviation for laugh out loud: to laugh at something because you think it is very funny
